verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचयतश्चान्तर्हितोऽनन्तस्तस्यै कृत्वा दिशे नम: ।विद्याधरश्चित्रकेतुश्चचार गगने चर: ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācayataś cāntarhito ’nantastasyai kṛtvā diśe namaḥvidyādharaś citraketuścacāra gagane caraḥ
verses.text: Śrīla Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: After offering obeisances to the direction from which Ananta, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, had disappeared, Citraketu began traveling in outer space as the head of the Vidyādharas.

verses.sanskrit: स लक्षं वर्षलक्षाणामव्याहतबलेन्द्रिय: ।स्तूयमानो महायोगी मुनिभि: सिद्धचारणै: ॥ २ ॥कुलाचलेन्द्रद्रोणीषु नानासङ्कल्पसिद्धिषु ।रेमे विद्याधरस्त्रीभिर्गापयन् हरिमीश्वरम् ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa lakṣaṁ varṣa-lakṣāṇāmavyāhata-balendriyaḥstūyamāno mahā-yogīmunibhiḥ siddha-cāraṇaiḥ
verses.text: Being praised by great sages and saints and by the inhabitants of Siddhaloka and Cāraṇaloka, Citraketu, the most powerful mystic yogī, wandered about enjoying life for millions of years. With bodily strength and senses free from deterioration, he traveled within the valleys of Sumeru Mountain, which is the place of perfection for various kinds of mystic power. In those valleys he enjoyed life with the women of Vidyādhara-loka by chanting the glories of the Supreme Lord, Hari.

verses.sanskrit: एकदा स विमानेन विष्णुदत्तेन भास्वता ।गिरिशं दद‍ृशे गच्छन् परीतं सिद्धचारणै: ॥ ४ ॥आलिङ्गय‍ाङ्कीकृतां देवीं बाहुना मुनिसंसदि ।उवाच देव्या: श‍ृण्वन्त्या जहासोच्चैस्तदन्तिके ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: ekadā sa vimānenaviṣṇu-dattena bhāsvatāgiriśaṁ dadṛśe gacchanparītaṁ siddha-cāraṇaiḥ
verses.text: One time while King Citraketu was traveling in outer space on a brilliantly effulgent airplane given to him by Lord Viṣṇu, he saw Lord Śiva, surrounded by Siddhas and Cāraṇas. Lord Śiva was sitting in an assembly of great saintly persons and embracing Pārvatī on his lap with his arm. Citraketu laughed loudly and spoke, within the hearing of Pārvatī.

verses.sanskrit: चित्रकेतुरुवाचएष लोकगुरु: साक्षाद्धर्मं वक्ता शरीरिणाम् ।आस्ते मुख्य: सभायां वै मिथुनीभूय भार्यया ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: citraketur uvācaeṣa loka-guruḥ sākṣāddharmaṁ vaktā śarīriṇāmāste mukhyaḥ sabhāyāṁ vaimithunī-bhūya bhāryayā
verses.text: Citraketu said: Lord Śiva, the spiritual master of the general populace, is the best of all living entities who have accepted material bodies. He enunciates the system of religion. Yet how wonderful it is that he is embracing his wife, Pārvatī, in the midst of an assembly of great saintly persons.

verses.sanskrit: जटाधरस्तीव्रतपा ब्रह्मवादिसभापति: ।अङ्कीकृत्य स्त्रियं चास्ते गतह्री: प्राकृतो यथा ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: jaṭā-dharas tīvra-tapābrahmavādi-sabhā-patiḥaṅkīkṛtya striyaṁ cāstegata-hrīḥ prākṛto yathā
verses.text: Lord Śiva, whose hair is matted on his head, has certainly undergone great austerities and penances. Indeed, he is the president in the assembly of strict followers of Vedic principles. Nonetheless, he is seated with his wife on his lap in the midst of saintly persons and is embracing her as if he were a shameless, ordinary human being.

verses.sanskrit: प्रायश: प्राकृताश्चापि स्त्रियं रहसि बिभ्रति ।अयं महाव्रतधरो बिभर्ति सदसि स्त्रियम् ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: prāyaśaḥ prākṛtāś cāpistriyaṁ rahasi bibhratiayaṁ mahā-vrata-dharobibharti sadasi striyam
verses.text: Ordinary conditioned persons generally embrace their wives and enjoy their company in solitary places. How wonderful it is that Lord Mahādeva, although a great master of austerity, is embracing his wife openly in the midst of an assembly of great saints.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचभगवानपि तच्छ्रुत्वा प्रहस्यागाधधीर्नृप ।तूष्णीं बभूव सदसि सभ्याश्च तदनुव्रता: ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācabhagavān api tac chrutvāprahasyāgādha-dhīr nṛpatūṣṇīṁ babhūva sadasisabhyāś ca tad-anuvratāḥ
verses.text: Śrīla Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued: My dear King, after hearing Citraketu’s statement, Lord Śiva, the most powerful personality, whose knowledge is fathomless, simply smiled and remained silent, and all the members of the assembly followed the lord by not saying anything.

verses.sanskrit: इत्यतद्वीर्यविदुषि ब्रुवाणे बह्वशोभनम् ।रुषाह देवी धृष्टाय निर्जितात्माभिमानिने ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: ity atad-vīrya-viduṣibruvāṇe bahv-aśobhanamruṣāha devī dhṛṣṭāyanirjitātmābhimānine
verses.text: Not knowing the prowess of Lord Śiva and Pārvatī, Citraketu strongly criticized them. His statements were not at all pleasing, and therefore the goddess Pārvatī, being very angry, spoke as follows to Citraketu, who thought himself better than Lord Śiva in controlling the senses.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीपार्वत्युवाचअयं किमधुना लोके शास्ता दण्डधर: प्रभु: ।अस्मद्विधानां दुष्टानां निर्लज्जानां च विप्रकृत् ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-pārvaty uvācaayaṁ kim adhunā lokeśāstā daṇḍa-dharaḥ prabhuḥasmad-vidhānāṁ duṣṭānāṁnirlajjānāṁ ca viprakṛt
verses.text: The goddess Pārvatī said: Alas, has this upstart now received a post from which to punish shameless persons like us? Has he been appointed ruler, carrier of the rod of punishment? Is he now the only master of everything?

verses.sanskrit: न वेद धर्मं किल पद्मयोनि-र्न ब्रह्मपुत्रा भृगुनारदाद्या: ।न वै कुमार: कपिलो मनुश्चये नो निषेधन्त्यतिवर्तिनं हरम् ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: na veda dharmaṁ kila padmayonirna brahma-putrā bhṛgu-nāradādyāḥna vai kumāraḥ kapilo manuś caye no niṣedhanty ati-vartinaṁ haram
verses.text: Alas, Lord Brahmā, who has taken his birth from the lotus flower, does not know the principles of religion, nor do the great saints like Bhṛgu and Nārada, nor the four Kumāras, headed by Sanat-kumāra. Manu and Kapila have also forgotten the religious principles. I suppose it to be because of this that they have not tried to stop Lord Śiva from behaving improperly.

verses.sanskrit: एषामनुध्येयपदाब्जयुग्मंजगद्गुरुं मङ्गलमङ्गलं स्वयम् ।य: क्षत्रबन्धु: परिभूय सूरीन्प्रशास्ति धृष्टस्तदयं हि दण्ड्य: ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: eṣām anudhyeya-padābja-yugmaṁjagad-guruṁ maṅgala-maṅgalaṁ svayamyaḥ kṣatra-bandhuḥ paribhūya sūrīnpraśāsti dhṛṣṭas tad ayaṁ hi daṇḍyaḥ
verses.text: This Citraketu is the lowest of kṣatriyas, for he has impudently overridden Brahmā and the other demigods by insulting Lord Śiva, upon whose lotus feet they always meditate. Lord Śiva is personified religion and the spiritual master of the entire world, and therefore Citraketu must be punished.

verses.sanskrit: नायमर्हति वैकुण्ठपादमूलोपसर्पणम् ।सम्भावितमति: स्तब्ध: साधुभि: पर्युपासितम् ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: nāyam arhati vaikuṇṭha-pāda-mūlopasarpaṇamsambhāvita-matiḥ stabdhaḥsādhubhiḥ paryupāsitam
verses.text: This person is puffed up because of his achievements, thinking, “I am the best.” He does not deserve to approach the shelter of Lord Viṣṇu’s lotus feet, which are worshiped by all saintly persons, for he is impudent, thinking himself greatly important.

verses.sanskrit: अत: पापीयसीं योनिमासुरीं याहि दुर्मते ।यथेह भूयो महतां न कर्ता पुत्र किल्बिषम् ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: ataḥ pāpīyasīṁ yonimāsurīṁ yāhi durmateyatheha bhūyo mahatāṁna kartā putra kilbiṣam
verses.text: O impudent one, my dear son, now take birth in a low, sinful family of demons so that you will not commit such an offense again toward exalted, saintly persons in this world.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचएवं शप्तश्चित्रकेतुर्विमानादवरुह्य स: ।प्रसादयामास सतीं मूर्ध्ना नम्रेण भारत ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaevaṁ śaptaś citraketurvimānād avaruhya saḥprasādayām āsa satīṁmūrdhnā namreṇa bhārata
verses.text: Śrī Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued: My dear King Parīkṣit, when Citraketu was cursed by Pārvatī, he descended from his airplane, bowed before her with great humility and pleased her completely.

verses.sanskrit: चित्रकेतुरुवाचप्रतिगृह्णामि ते शापमात्मनोऽञ्जलिनाम्बिके ।देवैर्मर्त्याय यत्प्रोक्तं पूर्वदिष्टं हि तस्य तत् ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: citraketur uvācapratigṛhṇāmi te śāpamātmano ’ñjalināmbikedevair martyāya yat proktaṁpūrva-diṣṭaṁ hi tasya tat
verses.text: Citraketu said: My dear mother, with my own hands folded together I accept the curse upon me. I do not mind the curse, for happiness and distress are given by the demigods as a result of one’s past deeds.

verses.sanskrit: संसारचक्र एतस्मिञ्जन्तुरज्ञानमोहित: ।भ्राम्यन् सुखं च दु:खं च भुङ्क्ते सर्वत्र सर्वदा ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: saṁsāra-cakra etasmiñjantur ajñāna-mohitaḥbhrāmyan sukhaṁ ca duḥkhaṁ cabhuṅkte sarvatra sarvadā
verses.text: Deluded by ignorance, the living entity wanders in the forest of this material world, enjoying the happiness and distress resulting from his past deeds, everywhere and at all times. [Therefore, my dear mother, neither you nor I am to be blamed for this incident.]

verses.sanskrit: नैवात्मा न परश्चापि कर्ता स्यात् सुखदु:खयो: ।कर्तारं मन्यतेऽत्राज्ञ आत्मानं परमेव च ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: naivātmā na paraś cāpikartā syāt sukha-duḥkhayoḥkartāraṁ manyate ’trājñaātmānaṁ param eva ca
verses.text: In this material world, neither the living entity himself nor others [friends and enemies] are the cause of material happiness and distress. But because of gross ignorance, the living entity thinks that he and others are the cause.

verses.sanskrit: गुणप्रवाह एतस्मिन् क: शाप: को न्वनुग्रह: ।क: स्वर्गो नरक: को वा किं सुखं दु:खमेव वा ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: guṇa-pravāha etasminkaḥ śāpaḥ ko nv anugrahaḥkaḥ svargo narakaḥ ko vākiṁ sukhaṁ duḥkham eva vā
verses.text: This material world resembles the waves of a constantly flowing river. Therefore, what is a curse and what is a favor? What are the heavenly planets, and what are the hellish planets? What is actually happiness, and what is actually distress? Because the waves flow constantly, none of them has an eternal effect.

verses.sanskrit: एक: सृजति भूतानि भगवानात्ममायया ।एषां बन्धं च मोक्षं च सुखं दु:खं च निष्कल: ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: ekaḥ sṛjati bhūtānibhagavān ātma-māyayāeṣāṁ bandhaṁ ca mokṣaṁ casukhaṁ duḥkhaṁ ca niṣkalaḥ
verses.text: The Supreme Personality of Godhead is one. Unaffected by the conditions of the material world, He creates all the conditioned souls by His own personal potency. Because of being contaminated by the material energy, the living entity is put into ignorance and thus into different conditions of bondage. Sometimes, by knowledge, the living entity is given liberation. In sattva-guṇa and rajo-guṇa, he is subjected to happiness and distress.

verses.sanskrit: न तस्य कश्चिद्दयित: प्रतीपोन ज्ञातिबन्धुर्न परो न च स्व: ।समस्य सर्वत्र निरञ्जनस्यसुखे न राग: कुत एव रोष: ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: na tasya kaścid dayitaḥ pratīpona jñāti-bandhur na paro na ca svaḥsamasya sarvatra nirañjanasyasukhe na rāgaḥ kuta eva roṣaḥ
verses.text: The Supreme Personality of Godhead is equally disposed toward all living entities. Therefore no one is very dear to Him, and no one is a great enemy for Him; no one is His friend, and no one is His relative. Being unattached to the material world, He has no affection for so-called happiness or hatred for so-called distress. The two terms happiness and distress are relative. Since the Lord is always happy, for Him there is no question of distress.

verses.sanskrit: तथापि तच्छक्तिविसर्ग एषांसुखाय दु:खाय हिताहिताय ।बन्धाय मोक्षाय च मृत्युजन्मनो:शरीरिणां संसृतयेऽवकल्पते ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tathāpi tac-chakti-visarga eṣāṁsukhāya duḥkhāya hitāhitāyabandhāya mokṣāya ca mṛtyu-janmanoḥśarīriṇāṁ saṁsṛtaye ’vakalpate
verses.text: Although the Supreme Lord is unattached to our happiness and distress according to karma, and although no one is His enemy or favorite, He creates pious and impious activities through the agency of His material potency. Thus for the continuation of the materialistic way of life He creates happiness and distress, good fortune and bad, bondage and liberation, birth and death.

verses.sanskrit: अथ प्रसादये न त्वां शापमोक्षाय भामिनि ।यन्मन्यसे ह्यसाधूक्तं मम तत्क्षम्यतां सति ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: atha prasādaye na tvāṁśāpa-mokṣāya bhāminiyan manyase hy asādhūktaṁmama tat kṣamyatāṁ sati
verses.text: O mother, you are now unnecessarily angry, but since all my happiness and distress are destined by my past activities, I do not plead to be excused or relieved from your curse. Although what I have said is not wrong, please let whatever you think is wrong be pardoned.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचइति प्रसाद्य गिरिशौ चित्रकेतुररिन्दम ।जगाम स्वविमानेन पश्यतो: स्मयतोस्तयो: ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaiti prasādya giriśaucitraketur arindamajagāma sva-vimānenapaśyatoḥ smayatos tayoḥ
verses.text: Śrī Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued: O King Parīkṣit, subduer of the enemy, after Citraketu satisfied Lord Śiva and his wife, Pārvatī, he boarded his airplane and left as they looked on. When Lord Śiva and Pārvatī saw that Citraketu, although informed of the curse, was unafraid, they smiled, being fully astonished by his behavior.

verses.sanskrit: ततस्तु भगवान् रुद्रो रुद्राणीमिदमब्रवीत् ।देवर्षिदैत्यसिद्धानां पार्षदानां च श‍ृण्वताम् ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatas tu bhagavān rudrorudrāṇīm idam abravītdevarṣi-daitya-siddhānāṁpārṣadānāṁ ca śṛṇvatām
verses.text: Thereafter, in the presence of the great sage Nārada, the demons, the inhabitants of Siddhaloka, and his personal associates, Lord Śiva, who is most powerful, spoke to his wife, Pārvatī, while they all listened.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीरुद्र उवाचद‍ृष्टवत्यसि सुश्रोणि हरेरद्भ‍ुतकर्मण: ।माहात्म्यं भृत्यभृत्यानां नि:स्पृहाणां महात्मनाम् ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-rudra uvācadṛṣṭavaty asi suśroṇiharer adbhuta-karmaṇaḥmāhātmyaṁ bhṛtya-bhṛtyānāṁniḥspṛhāṇāṁ mahātmanām
verses.text: Lord Śiva said: My dear beautiful Pārvatī, have you seen the greatness of the Vaiṣṇavas? Being servants of the servants of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Hari, they are great souls and are not interested in any kind of material happiness.

verses.sanskrit: नारायणपरा: सर्वे न कुतश्चन बिभ्यति ।स्वर्गापवर्गनरकेष्वपि तुल्यार्थदर्शिन: ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: nārāyaṇa-parāḥ sarvena kutaścana bibhyatisvargāpavarga-narakeṣvapi tulyārtha-darśinaḥ
verses.text: Devotees solely engaged in the devotional service of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Nārāyaṇa, never fear any condition of life. For them the heavenly planets, liberation and the hellish planets are all the same, for such devotees are interested only in the service of the Lord.

verses.sanskrit: देहिनां देहसंयोगाद् द्वन्द्वानीश्वरलीलया ।सुखं दु:खं मृतिर्जन्म शापोऽनुग्रह एव च ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: dehināṁ deha-saṁyogāddvandvānīśvara-līlayāsukhaṁ duḥkhaṁ mṛtir janmaśāpo ’nugraha eva ca
verses.text: Because of the actions of the Supreme Lord’s external energy, the living entities are conditioned in contact with material bodies. The dualities of happiness and distress, birth and death, curses and favors, are natural by-products of this contact in the material world.

verses.sanskrit: अविवेककृत: पुंसो ह्यर्थभेद इवात्मनि ।गुणदोषविकल्पश्च भिदेव स्रजिवत्कृत: ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: aviveka-kṛtaḥ puṁsohy artha-bheda ivātmaniguṇa-doṣa-vikalpaś cabhid eva srajivat kṛtaḥ
verses.text: As one mistakenly considers a flower garland to be a snake or experiences happiness and distress in a dream, so, in the material world, by a lack of careful consideration, we differentiate between happiness and distress, considering one good and the other bad.

verses.sanskrit: वासुदेवे भगवति भक्तिमुद्वहतां नृणाम् ।ज्ञानवैराग्यवीर्याणां न हि कश्चिद् व्यपाश्रय: ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: vāsudeve bhagavatibhaktim udvahatāṁ nṛṇāmjñāna-vairāgya-vīryāṇāṁna hi kaścid vyapāśrayaḥ
verses.text: Persons engaged in devotional service to Lord Vāsudeva, Kṛṣṇa, have naturally perfect knowledge and detachment from this material world. Therefore such devotees are not interested in the so-called happiness or so-called distress of this world.

verses.sanskrit: नाहं विरिञ्चो न कुमारनारदौन ब्रह्मपुत्रा मुनय: सुरेशा: ।विदाम यस्येहितमंशकांशकान तत्स्वरूपं पृथगीशमानिन: ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: nāhaṁ viriñco na kumāra-nāradauna brahma-putrā munayaḥ sureśāḥvidāma yasyehitam aṁśakāṁśakāna tat-svarūpaṁ pṛthag-īśa-māninaḥ
verses.text: Neither I [Lord Śiva], nor Brahmā, nor the Aśvinī-kumāras, nor Nārada or the other great sages who are Brahmā’s sons, nor even the demigods can understand the pastimes and personality of the Supreme Lord. Although we are part of the Supreme Lord, we consider ourselves independent, separate controllers, and thus we cannot understand His identity.

verses.sanskrit: न ह्यस्यास्ति प्रिय: कश्चिन्नाप्रिय: स्व: परोऽपि वा ।आत्मत्वात्सर्वभूतानां सर्वभूतप्रियो हरि: ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: na hy asyāsti priyaḥ kaścinnāpriyaḥ svaḥ paro ’pi vāātmatvāt sarva-bhūtānāṁsarva-bhūta-priyo hariḥ
verses.text: He holds no one as very dear and no one as inimical. He has no one for His own relative, and no one is alien to Him. He is actually the soul of the soul of all living entities. Thus He is the auspicious friend of all living beings and is very near and dear to all of them.

verses.sanskrit: तस्य चायं महाभागश्चित्रकेतु: प्रियोऽनुग: ।सर्वत्र समद‍ृक् शान्तो ह्यहं चैवाच्युतप्रिय: ॥ ३४ ॥तस्मान्न विस्मय: कार्य: पुरुषेषु महात्मसु ।महापुरुषभक्तेषु शान्तेषु समदर्शिषु ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasya cāyaṁ mahā-bhāgaścitraketuḥ priyo ’nugaḥsarvatra sama-dṛk śāntohy ahaṁ caivācyuta-priyaḥ
verses.text: This magnanimous Citraketu is a dear devotee of the Lord. He is equal to all living entities and is free from attachment and hatred. Similarly, I am also very dear to Lord Nārāyaṇa. Therefore, no one should be astonished to see the activities of the most exalted devotees of Nārāyaṇa, for they are free from attachment and envy. They are always peaceful, and they are equal to everyone.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचइति श्रुत्वा भगवत: शिवस्योमाभिभाषितम् ।बभूव शान्तधी राजन् देवी विगतविस्मया ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaiti śrutvā bhagavataḥśivasyomābhibhāṣitambabhūva śānta-dhī rājandevī vigata-vismayā
verses.text: Śrī Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: O King, after hearing this speech by her husband, the demigoddess [Umā, the wife of Lord Śiva] gave up her astonishment at the behavior of King Citraketu and became steady in intelligence.

verses.sanskrit: इति भागवतो देव्या: प्रतिशप्तुमलन्तम: ।मूर्ध्ना स जगृहे शापमेतावत्साधुलक्षणम् ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: iti bhāgavato devyāḥpratiśaptum alantamaḥmūrdhnā sa jagṛhe śāpametāvat sādhu-lakṣaṇam
verses.text: The great devotee Citraketu was so powerful that he was quite competent to curse mother Pārvatī in retaliation, but instead of doing so he very humbly accepted the curse and bowed his head before Lord Śiva and his wife. This is very much to be appreciated as the standard behavior of a Vaiṣṇava.

verses.sanskrit: जज्ञे त्वष्टुर्दक्षिणाग्नौ दानवीं योनिमाश्रित: ।वृत्र इत्यभिविख्यातो ज्ञानविज्ञानसंयुत: ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: jajñe tvaṣṭur dakṣiṇāgnaudānavīṁ yonim āśritaḥvṛtra ity abhivikhyātojñāna-vijñāna-saṁyutaḥ
verses.text: Being cursed by mother Durgā [Bhavānī, the wife of Lord Śiva], that same Citraketu accepted birth in a demoniac species of life. Although still fully equipped with transcendental knowledge and practical application of that knowledge in life, he appeared as a demon at the fire sacrifice performed by Tvaṣṭā, and thus he became famous as Vṛtrāsura.

verses.sanskrit: एतत्ते सर्वमाख्यातं यन्मां त्वं परिपृच्छसि ।वृत्रस्यासुरजातेश्च कारणं भगवन्मते: ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: etat te sarvam ākhyātaṁyan māṁ tvaṁ paripṛcchasivṛtrasyāsura-jāteś cakāraṇaṁ bhagavan-mateḥ
verses.text: My dear King Parīkṣit, you inquired from me how Vṛtrāsura, a great devotee, took birth in a demoniac family. Thus I have tried to explain to you everything about this.

verses.sanskrit: इतिहासमिमं पुण्यं चित्रकेतोर्महात्मन: ।माहात्म्यं विष्णुभक्तानां श्रुत्वा बन्धाद्विमुच्यते ॥ ४० ॥
verses.transliteration: itihāsam imaṁ puṇyaṁcitraketor mahātmanaḥmāhātmyaṁ viṣṇu-bhaktānāṁśrutvā bandhād vimucyate
verses.text: Citraketu was a great devotee [mahātmā]. If one hears this history of Citraketu from a pure devotee, the listener also is freed from the conditional life of material existence.

verses.sanskrit: य एतत्प्रातरुत्थाय श्रद्धया वाग्यत: पठेत् ।इतिहासं हरिं स्मृत्वा स याति परमां गतिम् ॥ ४१ ॥
verses.transliteration: ya etat prātar utthāyaśraddhayā vāg-yataḥ paṭhetitihāsaṁ hariṁ smṛtvāsa yāti paramāṁ gatim
verses.text: One who rises from bed early in the morning and recites this history of Citraketu, controlling his words and mind and remembering the Supreme Personality of Godhead, will return home, back to Godhead, without difficulty.
